name: outlook-local-mcp
description: Manage Microsoft Outlook calendars, events, and email directly from Claude. Runs entirely on your local machine — data stays on your device.
long_description:
A local MCP server that connects Claude to your Microsoft Outlook account via the Microsoft Graph API.

## Features

- **Calendar management**: list, create, update, delete, cancel, reschedule, and respond to events
- **Free/busy lookup**: check availability across time ranges
- **Email access**: list folders, search messages, read full message details
- **Multi-account**: add and switch between multiple Microsoft accounts
- **Recurrence support**: create and expand recurring events (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly)
- **Teams integration**: create events with automatic Teams online meeting links

## Privacy & Security

- Runs as a single binary on your machine — no cloud relay, no proxy
- OAuth tokens stored in your OS keychain (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager)
- Only connects to Microsoft Graph API with your explicit consent
- Delegated permissions only: Calendars.ReadWrite, Mail.Read, User.Read

tools.name: calendar
tools.description: Calendar operations for Microsoft Outlook via Microsoft Graph. Required operation: help (detailed docs), list_calendars (list user calendars), list_events (list events in a window), get_event (fetch one event), search_events (query events by subject and filters), create_event (personal event without attendees), update_event (edit personal event), delete_event (remove personal event), respond_event (accept/decline invitation), reschedule_event (move personal event), create_meeting (event with attendees), update_meeting (edit meeting with attendee changes), cancel_meeting (cancel and notify attendees), reschedule_meeting (move meeting and notify), get_free_busy (check availability).

tools.name: mail
tools.description: Mail operations for Microsoft Outlook via Microsoft Graph. Required operation: help (detailed docs), list_folders (list mail folders with unread counts), list_messages (list messages with filtering), get_message (get full message details), search_messages (KQL full-text search), get_conversation (fetch full thread chronologically — MailEnabled), list_attachments (list attachment metadata — MailEnabled), get_attachment (download attachment content — MailEnabled), create_draft (create new draft — MailManageEnabled), create_reply_draft (reply draft preserving threading — MailManageEnabled), create_forward_draft (forward draft with new recipients — MailManageEnabled), update_draft (update draft fields — MailManageEnabled), delete_draft (permanently delete a draft — MailManageEnabled).

tools.name: account
tools.description: Account management for Microsoft accounts. Required operation: help (detailed docs), add (add and authenticate a new account), remove (remove an account and clear tokens), list (list all accounts with state), login (re-authenticate a disconnected account), logout (disconnect without removing), refresh (force token refresh).

tools.name: system
tools.description: System diagnostics, authentication utilities, and embedded documentation access. Required operation: help (detailed docs), status (server health, accounts, config), complete_auth (finish auth_code flow — only when auth=auth_code), list_docs (list embedded documentation slugs), search_docs (keyword search across embedded docs), get_docs (fetch full content or a section by slug and anchor).

client_id.title: Microsoft Application (Client) ID
client_id.description: The app used to access Microsoft Graph. Default: 'outlook-desktop' (Microsoft Office, works out of the box with device_code auth). Use 'outlook-local-mcp' for the project's own app registration. To use browser auth, register your own app in Entra ID with http://localhost redirect URI and paste the client ID here.

tenant_id.title: Entra ID Tenant ID
tenant_id.description: Your Entra ID tenant. Use 'common' (default) for any Microsoft account, 'organizations' for work/school only, or 'consumers' for personal accounts only. Advanced: paste a specific tenant GUID to restrict access to a single organization.

auth_method.title: Authentication Method
auth_method.description: How you sign in to Microsoft. 'device_code' (default): visit a URL and enter a code — works everywhere, no app registration needed. 'browser': opens your browser automatically — requires your own app registration with http://localhost redirect URI. 'auth_code': manual flow for headless/remote environments.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Your timezone for calendar operations (IANA format). Use 'auto' to detect from your system, or specify explicitly, e.g. 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Tokyo'. Defaults to 'auto'.
